name: Spiced Beef & Potato Cakes
intro: This recipe is one of the simplest elements of a Persian feast, which may explain why it is a staple at many family parties, particularly around the time of the Persian New Year. The best and most popular way to eat these patties is actually the day after your party, stuffed into some bread with slices of tomato and gherkins. Either way, they are absolutely delicious and incredibly easy to make in large quantities. Feel free to adapt the recipe to your taste, substituting minced veal, lamb, chicken or turkey for the beef.

ingredients.item: breadcrumbs, blitzed to a fine consistency in a food processor
cooked potato, peeled, mashed and cooled
onion, minced in a food processor or very finely chopped
packet of coriander, stalks and leaves finely chopped

method: Pour the breadcrumbs on to a flat plate, ready for coating the patties.

Put the remaining ingredients, except for the oil, into a large mixing bowl and, using clean hands, mix thoroughly. You really need to employ your fingers and squelch through the mixture to ensure it is evenly combined and the egg and spices are evenly distributed.
Preheat a large nonstick frying pan over a medium-low heat if using gas, or a medium heat if cooking on electric, and preheat the oven on a low setting.

Take a ball of the mixture (slightly larger than a golf ball), roll it in between your palms and flatten it into a patty shape. Lay the patty gently into the breadcrumbs and coat each side with enough breadcrumbs to cover the surface on both sides. Set aside and repeat the process until all the patties are made and coated.

Drizzle a little oil into the preheated frying pan and fry several patties at a time without overcrowding the pan for 6–8 minutes on each side, or until nicely browned. Carefully remove them and drain on a plate lined with kitchen paper, then transfer to the oven and keep the cooked batches warm while you fry the remaining patties. Serve either with Persian Flatbread or on their own.
